examples.title: Boardwalk Rent-Share Contract Example
examples.summary: A player receives immediate cash in exchange for sharing future Boardwalk rent.
examples.text: Rent-share example: player-alpha pays player-beta 200 now, and player-beta owes player-alpha 25 percent of actual rent paid on Boardwalk for the next 4 turns. If Boardwalk rent is waived or unpaid, the rent-share follows the deterministic contract interaction rules.
validation_notes: The property_id must reference a valid property.
share_percent must be greater than 0 and no more than 100.
duration_turns must be a positive integer.

examples.title: Collateralized Railroad Loan Example
examples.summary: A short-term cash loan is secured by railroad collateral.
examples.text: Collateralized loan example: player-alpha lends player-beta 300 until turn 8. Reading Railroad and Pennsylvania Railroad are pledged as collateral for railroad-loan, and a default penalty references that loan for backend contract enforcement.
validation_notes: Collateral property ids must be valid properties.
The collateralized loan declares instrument_id railroad-loan.
The default penalty uses target_instrument_id to reference railroad-loan and must not reuse the loan instrument_id.

examples.title: Orange Group Purchase Option Example
examples.summary: A player buys the right to purchase an orange property later.
examples.text: Purchase option example: player-alpha pays for an option to buy Tennessee Avenue from player-beta for 260 before turn 12. The backend validates the strike price, expiration turn, property state, and transfer legality when the option is exercised.
validation_notes: The option property must be valid.
The holder and grantor must be distinct players.
Exercise still routes through backend validation.
